description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Michigan (  MISH-ig-ən) is a peninsular state in the Great Lakes region of the Upper Midwestern United States.

description_wiki: Crane High School may refer to one of the schools in the United States:

description: aerial warfare branch of the United States Army from 1941 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF or AAF) was the major land-based aerial warfare service component of the United States Army and de facto aerial warfare service branch of the United States during and immediately after World War II (1941–1947).

description: cemetery in St. Louis County, Missouri, USA
description_wiki: Jefferson Barracks National Cemetery is an American military cemetery located in St.

description: independent city in Missouri, United States
description_wiki: St.

description: city in Mississippi, USA
description_wiki: Gulfport ( GUHLF-port) is a port city in Harrison County, Mississippi, United States, and its co-county seat.

description: 1935 bomber aircraft family by Boeing
description_wiki: The Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress is an American four-engined heavy bomber aircraft that was developed in the mid-1930s for the United States Army Air Corps (USAAC).

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Maine (  MAYN) is a state in the New England region of the United States, and the northeasternmost state in the contiguous United States.

description: Nordic island country in the North Atlantic Ocean
description_wiki: Iceland is a Nordic island country between the Arctic Ocean and the North Atlantic Ocean, located on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge between Europe and North America.

description: chain of islands in the Northern Pacific
description_wiki: The Aleutian Islands (   ə-LOO-shən; Russian: Алеутские острова, romanized: Aleutskiye ostrova; Aleut: Unangam Tanangin, "land of the Aleuts"; possibly from the Chukchi aliat, or "island")—also called the Aleut Islands, Aleutic Islands, or, before the Alaska Purchase in 1867, the Catherine Archipelago—are a chain of 14 main, larger volcanic islands and 55 smaller ones.

description: island sovereign state off the coast of Southeast Africa, in the Indian Ocean
description_wiki: Madagascar, officially the Republic of Madagascar, is an island country in the Indian Ocean that includes the island of Madagascar and numerous smaller peripheral islands.

description: country in North Africa
description_wiki: Tunisia, officially the Republic of Tunisia, is a country in the Maghreb region of North Africa.

description: Italian comune
description_wiki: Foggia (UK: , US: ; Italian: [ˈfɔddʒa] ; Foggiano: Fògge [ˈfɔddʒə]) is a city and comune (municipality) of Apulia, in Southern Italy, capital of the province of Foggia.

description: capital city of Campania, Italy
description_wiki: Naples is the regional capital of Campania, Italy.

description: 1918–1992 country in Southeastern Europe
description_wiki: Yugoslavia (; lit. 'Land of the South Slavs') was a country in Central Europe and the Balkans that existed from 1918 to 1992.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Poland, officially the Republic of Poland, is a country in Central Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: Italian city, capital of the Apulia region
description_wiki: Bari ( BAR-ee, Italian: [ˈbaːri] ; Barese: Bare [ˈbæːrə]; Latin: Barium) is the capital city of the Metropolitan City of Bari and of the Apulia region, on the Adriatic Sea in southern Italy.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: Yugoslav revolutionary and statesman (1892−1980)
description_wiki: Josip Broz (7 May 1892 – 4 May 1980), commonly known as Tito ( TEE-toh), was a Yugoslav communist revolutionary and politician who led Yugoslavia as prime minister from 1943 to 1963 and as president from 1953 until his death in 1980.

description: African-American military pilots during World War II
description_wiki: The Tuskegee Airmen  were a group of primarily African-American military pilots (fighter and bomber) and airmen who fought in World War II. They formed the 332nd Fighter Group and the 477th Bombardment Group (Medium) of the United States Army Air Forces (USAAF).
